name: deepseek-autocomplete
description: Inline AI code suggestions powered by the DeepSeek API - ghost-text completions like GitHub Copilot, but using your own DeepSeek key.

deepseekAutocomplete.enabled.description: Show automatic inline (ghost-text) suggestions from DeepSeek as you type. You can still toggle this from the status bar or with the 'DeepSeek: Toggle Inline Suggestions' command.

deepseekAutocomplete.model.markdownDescription:
Which DeepSeek model to request completions from.

- `deepseek-v4-flash` - fast and cheap, recommended for inline autocomplete.
- `deepseek-v4-pro` - higher quality, slower and more expensive, useful if you want stronger suggestions and don't mind extra latency.

Legacy names `deepseek-chat` / `deepseek-reasoner` still work but are scheduled for retirement by DeepSeek and currently just alias to `deepseek-v4-flash`.

deepseekAutocomplete.baseUrl.description: Root URL of the DeepSeek-compatible API. Only change this if you are using a proxy or a self-hosted/OpenAI-compatible gateway in front of DeepSeek.

enumDescriptions: Fill-In-the-Middle: sends the code before and after the cursor to DeepSeek's /beta/completions endpoint. Fast, no chain-of-thought, closest to how editor autocomplete actually works.
Chat-style: wraps the surrounding code in a system/user prompt and calls /chat/completions. Use this if FIM ever misbehaves for your model/account.
deepseekAutocomplete.completionMode.description: How completions are requested from DeepSeek. 'fim' is recommended.

deepseekAutocomplete.enableThinking.description: Only used when completionMode is 'chat'. DeepSeek V4 has 'thinking mode' on by default, which adds noticeable latency and cost - keep this off for snappy inline suggestions. Has no effect in 'fim' mode.

deepseekAutocomplete.maxTokens.description: Maximum number of tokens DeepSeek may generate for a single suggestion. Smaller is faster; larger allows longer multi-line suggestions.

deepseekAutocomplete.temperature.description: Sampling temperature. Lower values (0–0.3) give more predictable, conservative completions, which is usually what you want for code.

deepseekAutocomplete.debounceMs.description: How long to wait after you stop typing before asking DeepSeek for a suggestion. Higher values reduce API usage but feel less responsive.

deepseekAutocomplete.requestTimeoutMs.description: How long to wait for a DeepSeek response before giving up on a suggestion.

deepseekAutocomplete.contextLinesBefore.description: Number of lines before the cursor (in the current file) to send as context.

deepseekAutocomplete.contextLinesAfter.description: Number of lines after the cursor (in the current file) to send as context.

deepseekAutocomplete.includeOpenFiles.description: Include short snippets from your other open editor tabs as extra context, similar to Copilot's 'neighboring tabs'. Helps DeepSeek match patterns, imports, and naming used elsewhere in your codebase.

deepseekAutocomplete.maxContextFiles.description: Maximum number of other open files to include as context (only used when includeOpenFiles is true). Files matching the current file's language are preferred.

deepseekAutocomplete.maxContextCharsPerF.description: Maximum characters taken from each neighboring open file used as context.

deepseekAutocomplete.includeClipboard.description: Include the current system clipboard content as extra context, prepended ahead of the current file. Useful when you've copied relevant code, API signatures, or reference snippets.

deepseekAutocomplete.maxClipboardChars.description: Maximum characters taken from the clipboard when includeClipboard is enabled.

deepseekAutocomplete.disabledLanguages.description: Language IDs to never show suggestions for (e.g. "plaintext", "markdown").
